question: Does the appreciation model generalize beyond the qualities it was trained on?

zero_shot_to_never_trained_tokens.setup: Hold qualities out of TRAINING; test whether the model can still produce a valid appreciation for them.

attempts.name: tokenizer-seed only (embeddings never trained)
attempts.why: A held-out token's embedding gets no gradient, so a tied-embedding model can't emit it; it substitutes a trained quality (e.g. 'Topic: wisdom' -> 'your calmness ...'). Held at 24- and 205-quality scales.

attempts.name: vocabulary warm-up (held-out words exposed in a neutral list so embeddings train)
attempts.why: Growing the embeddings was NOT enough: the model STILL substitutes a trained quality (e.g. 'Topic: wit' -> 'your dynamism ...', 'Topic: wisdom' -> 'your stewardship ...'). So the model never formed a copy/induction head; it MEMORIZES per-quality and has no mechanism to route an unseen quality from the cue into the answer. The limitation is architectural, not just embedding-norm.

zero_shot_to_never_trained_tokens.honest_conclusion: Zero-shot generalization to a quality the model never saw in an appreciation is ~0% for this tiny template architecture. Two principled fixes were tried and measured; both stayed at 0%. Genuinely fixing it would need an explicit copy/pointer mechanism or far more scale — out of scope for a ~5-14M template model. Reported as a negative result, not hidden.

coverage_generalization.setup: Train on a much larger, diverse quality vocabulary so every quality the model is asked about is trained.
coverage_generalization.why: Expanding from 24 to 229 vetted single-word qualities makes the model generalize across a 10x larger, diverse quality space — all 229 produce valid, wholesome appreciation. This is the achievable, useful form of generalization for a tiny word-level model.

note: Measured, not claimed. The shipped model erosolar-v0.01 trains on all 229 qualities; the held-out runs are separate diagnostics. Neither finding is a capability-class claim.
